title: Fawn Response
thesis: The fourth trauma response. The one most people never name. A five-stage protocol to stop running it — sourced strictly from peer-reviewed work.
meta_description: The fourth trauma response: appease. What it looks like, why it forms, and a five-stage protocol to stop running it. Sourced from The Self-Care You Were Never Taught, Chapter 4.
og_description: The fourth trauma response. The five-stage protocol to stop running it.
twitter_description: The fourth trauma response. The five-stage protocol to stop running it.
schema_keywords: fawn response, trauma response, Pete Walker, complex PTSD, schema therapy, internal family systems, assertiveness

pull_quote.text: Fawn types seek safety by merging with the wishes, needs, and demands of others. They act as if they unconsciously believe that the price of admission to any relationship is the forfeiture of all their needs, rights, preferences, and boundaries.
pull_quote.cite: — Pete Walker, Complex PTSD: From Surviving to Thriving (2013)

steps.name: Tracking
steps.body: You start noticing the fawn response in real time.

steps.name: Graduated Exposure
steps.body: Speed et al. (2017) framed assertiveness training around a 0-100 SUDS scale.

steps.name: Integration
steps.body: The new behaviors stop feeling effortful.

steps.name: Relapse and Repair
steps.body: You will fall back into fawn patterns. Guaranteed.

items.help: Genuine generosity feels expansive. Fawning feels tight, pressured, resigned.

items.num: 02 · Can I say no without rehearsing?
items.text: Try the no in your head, plain and unjustified.
items.help: If the no needs a script, an excuse, or extensive justification, the yes was probably fear-based.

items.text: Imagine yourself doing the thing. Watch your gut.
items.help: Resentment is the signature emotion of the fawn response.

items.num: 04 · Would I do this if no one knew?
items.help: Fawning often requires an audience. Kindness usually doesn't.

sources_intro: All claims on this page are cited in The Self-Care You Were Never Taught, Chapter 4 (pp. 48-59).
